creator: timp87@gmail.com
text:
Great! Thanks a lot!
I'd like the old net-mgmt/flow-tools to desappear from ports too =)

2013/11/20 Muhammad Moinur Rahman <5u623l20@gmail.com>:
> http://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/4.1/en/my-init.html
> I think you might be running a threadsafe version of mysql that's why it is
> showing yes.
>
> I have already submitted a patch for upgrading the flow-tools-ng with the
> new OPTIONS framework which will show you an option to configure various
> aspects. However as the old OPTION framework you can try to do the
> following:
> # cd /usr/ports/net-mgmt/flow-tools-ng
> # make install clean -DWITH_MYSQL
>
> FYI .. http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=184115
>
> Wait for a couple of days, I am working on upgrading flowviewer. :)
>
>
> On Wed, Nov 20, 2013 at 1:58 PM, Pavel Timofeev <timp87@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>> > Hi, Muhammad Moinur Rahman!
>> >
>> > I looked at logs from 10.0 and it look strange.
>> >
>> > I saw this line in your log
>> > "checking for my_init in -lmysqlclient... no"
>> >
>> > What does it mean?
>> >
>> > Because my log contains
>> > "checking for my_init in -lmysqlclient... yes"
>>
>> And, yes, I'd like to use flow-tools-ng instead, but there are some
>> ports that conected flow-tools.
>> For example, net-mgmt/flowviewer (which is not up to date).
>> And flow-tools-ng has some problems such as
>> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=183782
>
>
